title: Abildgaardia indica
taxonomy: Abildgaardia indica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, which includes sedges and rushes. This plant is a member of the genus Abildgaardia, which is a group of plants native to Asia and Africa. Abildgaardia indica is further classified as a monocotyledon, a type of plant that has one cotyledon or seed leaf in its seedling stage. The plant's classification is based on its characteristics, such as its leaves, roots, and inflorescences. Abildgaardia indica is a specific epithet within the larger classification of the plant kingdom.

intro: Abildgaardia indica, also known as Indian marsh cress or water cress, is a semi-aquatic plant species belonging to the family Zosteraceae.
